codeReview.summary: The PostgresCheckpointStore implementation is well-structured with event-sourced append-only schema, event chaining for audit trail, and materialized views for efficient state reconstruction. All CheckpointStore trait methods are implemented. Test coverage includes 9 tests (5 unit, 4 integration) covering event types, hash computation, serialization, and CRUD operations.

issues.description: Hash computation uses DefaultHasher (SipHash) instead of a cryptographic hash (e.g., SHA-256). While adequate for non-repudiation event ordering, production audit trails typically use cryptographic hashes. This is acceptable for the current MVP but should be noted.

refresh_materialized_views does not use CONCURRENTLY option. In production with high write volume, this blocks concurrent writes during refresh. Consider using REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY for production use.
CheckpointEventType enum uses VARCHAR(50) but there is no DB index on event_type column. Queries filtering by event type may require full scans.
prune() with keep=0 returns early without appending any event. Callers expecting a prune event recorded may be surprised. Consider documenting this behavior or always appending a prune event.

Test function names use 'test_' prefix (e.g., test_checkpoint_event_type_as_str) which is redundant with Rust's #[test] attribute. Standard convention is to name tests without the 'test_' prefix (e.g., checkpoint_event_type_as_str).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Test function naming convention deviates from standard Rust. The codebase uses 'test_' prefix on function names (e.g., test_checkpoint_event_type_as_str) but standard Rust convention omits the prefix since #[test] already indicates it's a test.

The transcript shows worker used 'rust-worker' skill which doesn't exist in .factory/skills/. The actual skill is 'rust' (with no '-worker' suffix). Worker worked around this by proceeding with base worker procedure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'The rust-worker skill is not found. Per the mission-worker-base procedure, I should return to the orchestrator immediately.' - but worker proceeded anyway. The skill path is .factory/skills/rust/ not rust-worker.

summary: PostgresCheckpointStore implementation passes review with solid event-sourced architecture. The implementation includes: (1) append-only checkpoint_events table with event chaining via previous_hash/event_hash, (2) CheckpointEventType enum with CheckpointCreated/CheckpointUpdated/CheckpointsPruned variants, (3) two materialized views (latest_task_checkpoints, task_state_history), and (4) all CheckpointStore trait methods. Minor observations: DefaultHasher used instead of cryptographic hash, no CONCURRENTLY on materialized view refresh, and test naming style deviation from standard Rust conventions.
